title: Introduce RAG cold-start warmup quickstart with edge prefetch and wasm preprocessor
preview: Motivation: cold-start latency is a common pain for edge-deployed RAG microservices where first requests trigger model warming and index priming; this quickstart demonstrates an opinionated pattern (edge prefetch + lightweight WASM request preprocessor + adaptive prefix cache) to reduce first-request tail latency wi...
text: Motivation: cold-start latency is a common pain for edge-deployed RAG microservices where first requests trigger model warming and index priming; this quickstart demonstrates an opinionated pattern (edge prefetch + lightweight WASM request preprocessor + adaptive prefix cache) to reduce first-request tail latency without changing server-side runtime. What this PR adds: a new quickstart 'rag-coldstart-warmup' with README, a small TypeScript edge worker example that prefetches likely context, a WASM preprocessor Dockerfile and build scaffold, sample Terraform module to deploy to an edge Lambda/edge VM, a small indexed sample corpus and manifest.yaml, and a GH Actions smoke job that validates end-to-end cold-start latency under controlled conditions. Checklist: [x] Quickstart README with architecture diagram and step-by-step run instructions; [x] Local dev script (scripts/run-local.sh) t...

title: introduce-unified-rag-starterkit-multilang-bindings-and-docsite-snippets
preview: Motivation: Customers repeatedly asked for a compact, multi-language RAG starter that is small enough to fork as a template but comprehensive enough to cover model selection, embeddings, caching, and observability. This PR introduces a unified starter kit that: 1) provides TypeScript, Python, and Go reference runner...
text:
Motivation: Customers repeatedly asked for a compact, multi-language RAG starter that is small enough to fork as a template but comprehensive enough to cover model selection, embeddings, caching, and observability. This PR introduces a unified starter kit that: 1) provides TypeScript, Python, and Go reference runners; 2) includes small telemetry adapter shims that wire into Redwood Console probes; 3) adds docsite snippets and quickstart exercises so users can get from zero to a running RAG demo in under 10 minutes.

What changed (high level): - new starter directory with language-specific runners and minimal infra (docker-compose + local index) - telemetry adapters that emit the same semantic events we use in the Console (session_start, retrieval_hit, model_call, latency_bucket) - compact README and step-by-step Quickstart docs added to the docs snippets directory - small helper libra...

title: introduce-modular-rag-studio-lite-quickstart
preview: Adds a compact, modular RAG Studio quickstart aimed at developer demos and offline authoring. Motivation: we frequently field requests for a lightweight, dependency-minimal sandbox that demonstrates tool-calling, incremental indexing, and scenario-driven evaluations without needing heavy infra. This quickstart targe...
text: Adds a compact, modular RAG Studio quickstart aimed at developer demos and offline authoring. Motivation: we frequently field requests for a lightweight, dependency-minimal sandbox that demonstrates tool-calling, incremental indexing, and scenario-driven evaluations without needing heavy infra. This quickstart targets two personas: 1) engineers who want a reproducible local demo for product meetings and 2) contributors who need an isolated harness for writing scenario-based evals. Change summary: 1) new CLI scaffolder (bin/mini-rag) that bootstraps a tiny web UI, an in-memory incremental indexer, and a WASM-based tool stub runtime; 2) compact devcontainer and Docker profile for one-command local runs; 3) a small set of authored scenarios (samples/) with step assertions and a recorder that captures model/tool interactions for playback; 4) lightweight telemetry stub that emits structure...

title: Add introspective RAG playground + debugger walkthrough and probe adapters
preview:
Context and motivation:
This quickstart helps engineers debug RAG pipelines end-to-end in a reproducible local environment. Teams building retrieval-augmented features have asked for better visibility into where failures and hallucinations originate (retriever, index, ranking, or model generation). The aim of this P...
text:
Context and motivation:
This quickstart helps engineers debug RAG pipelines end-to-end in a reproducible local environment. Teams building retrieval-augmented features have asked for better visibility into where failures and hallucinations originate (retriever, index, ranking, or model generation). The aim of this PR is to provide a small, opinionated playground that surfaces retrieval traces, prefix/KV cache snapshots, and token-level model call traces — while keeping everything runnable with a single devcontainer + smoke CI job.

What this PR adds (high-level):
- A new quickstart workspace 'rag-introspection' with README-guided walkthrough and sample corpora.
- Devcontainer configuration for instant onboarding and preinstalled tooling (Python, Node, yarn, poetry).
- Two minimal runners: a TypeScript 'playground-runner' and a Python 'play-run' that demonstrate instrumented calls to t...

title: Introduce snapshot-branch authoring quickstart for iterative RAG scenario branching
preview: Problem: teams authoring RAG scenarios frequently need to iterate variations of prompts, tool bindings, and small index changes in isolation and then merge the best variants back into a canonical scenario. Existing quickstarts show single-file examples or full-playground runners but lack a lightweight, reproducible ...
text:
Problem: teams authoring RAG scenarios frequently need to iterate variations of prompts, tool bindings, and small index changes in isolation and then merge the best variants back into a canonical scenario. Existing quickstarts show single-file examples or full-playground runners but lack a lightweight, reproducible branching workflow for local authoring and telemetry-driven selection.

What this PR adds: a Snapshot-Branch Quickstart that provides a simple authoring loop: capture a snapshot of the current scenario, create a branch, run iterative edits (prompts, tool contracts, index seed), record deterministic telemetry for each run, and merge a chosen snapshot back into the main scenario. The quickstart is intentionally minimal: no server required, runs locally with the Redwood CLI and the quickstart runner, and includes a devcontainer for reproducible environments.

Key features: sna...

title: Introduce stepwise RAG authoring kiosk and Go agent examples with telemetry probes
preview: This PR adds a compact stepwise authoring "kiosk" quickstart for building RAG workflows and a minimal Go-based example agent that demonstrates tool-calling, structured output, and telemetry probes. Motivation: provide a low-friction, reproducible onboarding path for engineers who want to prototype multi-step retriev...
text: This PR adds a compact stepwise authoring "kiosk" quickstart for building RAG workflows and a minimal Go-based example agent that demonstrates tool-calling, structured output, and telemetry probes. Motivation: provide a low-friction, reproducible onboarding path for engineers who want to prototype multi-step retrieval workflows locally and validate them with lightweight step assertions. The kiosk is intentionally opinionated: it exposes a guided step sequencer, live index preview, and a watch-mode that mirrors edits to a local authoring file. The Go sample agent demonstrates secure tool invocation patterns, streaming handling, and a small telemetry shim that emits step-level events compatible with Redwood Console. Checklist: - [x] Add stepwise authoring UI quickstart with README, run instructions, and configuration templates - [x] Add Go sample agent with tool stubs and example tests ...

title: Add RAG Orchestration Smokehouse walkthrough and reproducible smoke CI
preview: Motivation: provide a compact, reproducible authoring + test walkthrough for building RAG-based orchestrations that call external tools. Many teams ask for a single, batteries-included example that covers: index seeding, simple tool-binding (HTTP webhook + local mock), stepwise agent execution, and a smoke CI that c...
text:
Motivation: provide a compact, reproducible authoring + test walkthrough for building RAG-based orchestrations that call external tools. Many teams ask for a single, batteries-included example that covers: index seeding, simple tool-binding (HTTP webhook + local mock), stepwise agent execution, and a smoke CI that can run locally or in CI to catch regressions to tool-calling and structured output.

What this PR adds (summary):
- A lightweight ‘smokehouse’ quickstart under quickstarts/rag-smokehouse/ that includes: README, seed index script, tiny in-process tool-mock server, example orchestration YAML, and an idempotent smoke CI runner.
- Two example agent patterns: stepwise-orchestrator and event-driven-saga (small, focused code + config in TypeScript).
- A reproducible smoke CI harness (node-based) that runs against a pinned dev model variant and asserts structured outputs for three ...

title: Introduce RAG rehearsal sandbox with step-mocks and eval hooks
preview: Motivation: Add a small, local-first RAG "rehearsal" quickstart that helps engineers and evaluators iterate on end-to-end scenarios deterministically. The sandbox provides: 1) a scenario YAML format for stepwise interactions (queries, tool calls, agent decisions), 2) step-mock harnesses to replace external tools (se...
text:
Motivation: Add a small, local-first RAG "rehearsal" quickstart that helps engineers and evaluators iterate on end-to-end scenarios deterministically. The sandbox provides: 1) a scenario YAML format for stepwise interactions (queries, tool calls, agent decisions), 2) step-mock harnesses to replace external tools (search, embeddings, vector stores) with canned responses, 3) a lightweight CLI runner to replay scenarios locally and capture structured traces, and 4) eval hooks that emit run-level metrics and Golden-expected assertions for CI integration.

This PR introduces the example project, docs, and a test harness.

Checklist:
- [x] Add quickstart scaffolding and README
- [x] Implement scenario schema and runner CLI
- [x] Provide three sample scenarios (happy path, ambiguous retrieval, safety-fallback)
- [x] Add step-mock implementations for search/knn/embeddings
- [x] Integrate tele...

title: introduce-rag-llm-bridge-authoring-slate-quickstart
preview:
Context and motivation: Teams building retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) workflows often need a minimal, opinionated authoring surface that ties together prompt templates, tool contracts, structured output validators, and live telemetry presets. This quickstart ("LLM Bridge Authoring Slate"

text:
Context and motivation: Teams building retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) workflows often need a minimal, opinionated authoring surface that ties together prompt templates, tool contracts, structured output validators, and live telemetry presets. This quickstart ("LLM Bridge Authoring Slate"

What this PR adds: a compact developer playground and recipe that demonstrates: 1) contract-first tool declarations (OpenAPI/JSON Schema style) that the RAG runtime can call safely; 2) a lightweight authoring slate UI for editing prompt templates and live-previewing structured outputs; 3) a small sample indexer and retrieval shim (sqlite + simple vectorizer) for local experiments; 4) telemetry presets (token/response latency breakdown, tool-call traces, schema validation failures) wired to the Console dev-mode; and 5) a step-by-step README and end-to-end smoke tests for CI.

title: introduce-stateful-rag-finite-state-machine-quickstart
preview: Context & motivation: Many teams want RAG flows that maintain explicit conversational state across turns (session phases, latent tool state, multi-step confirmations) without shipping a heavyweight orchestrator. This PR introduces a compact Quickstart that wires a finite-state-machine (FSM) pattern to Redwood's tool...
text: Context & motivation: Many teams want RAG flows that maintain explicit conversational state across turns (session phases, latent tool state, multi-step confirmations) without shipping a heavyweight orchestrator. This PR introduces a compact Quickstart that wires a finite-state-machine (FSM) pattern to Redwood's tool-calling and incremental index APIs so developers can bootstrap stateful RAG sessions with clear guardrails and reproducible telemetry. Summary of changes: 1) new quickstart: quickstarts/stateful-fsm-rag/ containing a README, TypeScript sample runner, an auth-free local dev shim, and a test harness; 2) authoring doc: docs/authoring/stateful-rag-fsm.md with design rationale and recommended prompt templates; 3) telemetry presets: quickstarts/stateful-fsm-rag/telemetry-presets.json for Console probe ingestion; 4) smoke CI workflow: .github/workflows/qst-stateful-fsm-smoke.yml ...

title: Add snippet-explorer worklet with typed telemetry hooks and runnable README
preview: Motivation: shipping compact, runnable quickstarts that demonstrate structured tool-calling, streaming snippets, and minimal telemetry hooks has been a frequent ask from customer-facing teams. This PR introduces a new quickstart called 'snippet-explorer' — a small web worklet + Node runner that shows how to wire Red...
text:
Motivation: shipping compact, runnable quickstarts that demonstrate structured tool-calling, streaming snippets, and minimal telemetry hooks has been a frequent ask from customer-facing teams. This PR introduces a new quickstart called 'snippet-explorer' — a small web worklet + Node runner that shows how to wire Redwood SDK streaming, tool calls, and typed telemetry probes into a developer playground.

What this PR contains: a) quickstarts/snippet-explorer/README.md with a one-minute intro, runnable steps (devcontainer, npm start, and a single smoke request). b) playlet/index.ts: lightweight worklet that runs a sample agent, exposes a compact UI stub and emits typed probe events. c) examples/snippet-explorer/telemetry-probes.yaml: example probe schema and recommended labels for integration with Console ingestion. d) docs/quickstarts/snippet-explorer.md: docs-site-ready copy with API s...

title: Add RAG telemetry playback minirunner quickstart and docs
preview: Motivation: Reproducible debugging of RAG sessions requires the ability to replay request/response and telemetry traces locally. This quickstart introduces a lightweight "minirunner" that replays recorded telemetry and request traces into a local RAG dev environment so engineers and SREs can reproduce quality regres...
text:
Motivation: Reproducible debugging of RAG sessions requires the ability to replay request/response and telemetry traces locally. This quickstart introduces a lightweight "minirunner" that replays recorded telemetry and request traces into a local RAG dev environment so engineers and SREs can reproduce quality regressions, latency spikes, and tool-calling failures without needing a full cluster.

What this PR adds: a) a minimal Go-based playback worker (examples/playback-minirunner/) that consumes Redwood-formatted trace bundles and emits the same sequence of API calls and tool invocations to a developer's local stack; b) a small TypeScript shim (examples/ts-playback-proxy/) that simulates the tool adapter surface for browser examples; c) a step-by-step quickstart (quickstarts/rag-telemetry-playback/README.md) with sample trace bundles, CLI usage, and troubleshooting tips; d) docs site...

title: Introduce RAG Navigator: lightweight interactive playground and CLI walkthrough
preview: Context and motivation: teams keep asking for a tiny, batteries-included RAG playground that lets new engineers run a retrieval+agent loop locally with minimal setup and observable telemetry. This PR adds 'RAG Navigator', a lightweight quickstart that combines: 1) a minimal Node-based CLI to scaffold an index and se...
text: Context and motivation: teams keep asking for a tiny, batteries-included RAG playground that lets new engineers run a retrieval+agent loop locally with minimal setup and observable telemetry. This PR adds 'RAG Navigator', a lightweight quickstart that combines: 1) a minimal Node-based CLI to scaffold an index and seed prompts; 2) a small devserver that serves a compact interactive UI for stepwise inspection of agent decisions and tool calls; 3) opinionated telemetry probes that emit structured traces (local file/console) for step-level assertions; 4) example prompt templates and a guided walkthrough README. Checklist: - [x] scaffold CLI and devserver - [x] add telemetry probes and sample assertions - [x] provide two example corpora (kb-small, kb-news) - [x] include CI smoke tests and offline index fixture - [x] add docs quickstart page and links to examples gallery Implementation note...

title: add-interactive-rag-kata-telemetry-workbench-and-authoring-sandbox-quickstart
preview: Context: New developer-onboarding quickstart to accelerate RAG authoring and telemetry adoption. The goal is to provide a compact, hands-on sandbox with stepwise exercises (katas) that teach: setting up a local index, wiring a retrieval tool, authoring prompts with structured output checks, and instrumenting telemet...
text:
Context: New developer-onboarding quickstart to accelerate RAG authoring and telemetry adoption. The goal is to provide a compact, hands-on sandbox with stepwise exercises (katas) that teach: setting up a local index, wiring a retrieval tool, authoring prompts with structured output checks, and instrumenting telemetry probes for latency/cost/quality. This PR delivers the sandbox app, three kata scenarios, a telemetry probes package, docs site updates, and CI smoke tests.

Motivation: Engineering teams repeatedly ask for reproducible, minimal examples focused on both correctness (assertions/step checks) and observability. Existing quickstarts teach authoring but omit live telemetry wiring or stepwise kata-style learning. This PR fills that gap and provides a low-friction path to try telemetry workbench features paired with RAG.

Checklist: - [x] Add interactive sandbox app with live-re...

title: Add RAG tool-bound workflow quickstart with stubbed agent runtime
preview: Motivation: provide a small, battery-included quickstart for teams authoring retrieval-augmented tool-calling agents that need fast local feedback loops. This quickstart packages: 1) a tiny stubbed agent runtime (TypeScript) that runs locally and exposes a tool-binding API; 2) a set of contract-first tool schemas an...
text: Motivation: provide a small, battery-included quickstart for teams authoring retrieval-augmented tool-calling agents that need fast local feedback loops. This quickstart packages: 1) a tiny stubbed agent runtime (TypeScript) that runs locally and exposes a tool-binding API; 2) a set of contract-first tool schemas and structured-output validators; 3) an incremental index + mocked retriever that can be swapped for a real vector DB; 4) telemetry probe scaffolds and a smoke test harness for CI. The goal is to let engineers iterate on tool contracts and prompt/tool orchestration without provisioning full infra or real connectors. What this PR changes: - Adds quickstarts/toolbound-agent/README.md with step-by-step authoring, troubleshooting, and intended learning objectives. - Adds an opinionated example agent at quickstarts/toolbound-agent/examples/agent.ts demonstrating tool discovery, de...

title: Add “Hello world” first request snippet aligned with activation CTAs
preview:
## Context
This sprint’s primary activation KPI is “first successful request” and “first 100k tokens” within 7 days. We’ve seen drop-off at the earliest step due to (a) too many options in the quickstart, (b) mismatch between lifecycle email CTAs and what the docs show, and (c) missing/unclear env var and model defa...
text:
## Context
This sprint’s primary activation KPI is “first successful request” and “first 100k tokens” within 7 days. We’ve seen drop-off at the earliest step due to (a) too many options in the quickstart, (b) mismatch between lifecycle email CTAs and what the docs show, and (c) missing/unclear env var and model defaults.

This PR adds a minimal, copy/paste “Hello world” snippet that matches the onboarding email v2 + console onboarding checklist CTA wording: “Make your first request.” The snippet is intentionally small: create API key → export env var → single streaming request.

## What changed
- New `hello-world` page that is optimized for TTFT (goal: <2 minutes from API key creation to first streamed token).
- Adds 3 variants with identical structure:
 - curl (streaming)
 - Node/TypeScript (SDK)
 - Python (SDK)
- Standardizes naming used across growth surfaces:
 - Env var: `REDWOOD_...

title: Add RAG assertion workshop and offline eval kit
preview: Motivation: Engineers building retrieval-augmented agents need reproducible, offline workflows to author prompt/knowledge assertions and validate regressions without spinning up hosted infra. This quickstart provides a compact workshop that demonstrates assertion-driven evals, example agent tool-calling patterns, an...
text:
Motivation: Engineers building retrieval-augmented agents need reproducible, offline workflows to author prompt/knowledge assertions and validate regressions without spinning up hosted infra. This quickstart provides a compact workshop that demonstrates assertion-driven evals, example agent tool-calling patterns, and telemetry probes that map errors to actionable traces.

What this PR adds: - a guided workshop README with hands-on tasks and expected outcomes - a tiny assertion DSL and runner (authorable JSON/YAML assertion files) to express functional and quality checks - an offline eval harness that spins a local retriever + lightweight model shim for fast iteration - telemetry probe presets (span names, tags, and sampling hints) and a small dashboard template - CI smoke job to run the assertion suite on PRs so regressions are caught early

Key usage notes: 1) Clone the quickstart an...

title: interactive rag scenarios: tool timeouts and resilient fallbacks quickstart
preview: Adds an interactive quickstart demonstrating robust RAG agent patterns when calling external tools: per-tool timeout and retry policies, deterministic fallback strategies (local cache / degraded-mode answers), session replay for authoring and debugging, and lightweight telemetry probes for latency-sensitive deployme...
text: Adds an interactive quickstart demonstrating robust RAG agent patterns when calling external tools: per-tool timeout and retry policies, deterministic fallback strategies (local cache / degraded-mode answers), session replay for authoring and debugging, and lightweight telemetry probes for latency-sensitive deployments. The quickstart includes a compact dev server to run scenarios locally, stepwise assertions for authoring test cases, and CI smoke tests that validate timeouts and fallback behavior. Motivated by ENG-1843 and on-call feedback about tool stalls increasing MTTR; this quickstart is aimed at engineers evaluating production guardrails for tool-calling agents and improves observability during local dev and CI verification.

title: Introduce RAG step-factory playbook and lightweight e2e devkit
preview: Context and motivation: Teams repeatedly ask for a compact, production-adjacent RAG quickstart that (1) defines reusable step templates for common pipeline stages (query rewrite, candidate fetch, rerank, synthesis), (2) ships a tiny devkit for deterministic e2e runs and replayable telemetry, and (3) demonstrates a c...
text: Context and motivation: Teams repeatedly ask for a compact, production-adjacent RAG quickstart that (1) defines reusable step templates for common pipeline stages (query rewrite, candidate fetch, rerank, synthesis), (2) ships a tiny devkit for deterministic e2e runs and replayable telemetry, and (3) demonstrates a connector pattern (GraphQL) used in real products. This PR introduces the 'step-factory' playbook: a curated set of YAML step templates, a node-based devkit runner with a lightweight in-memory index/replay harness, and docs + smoke CI to make authoring and testing RAG flows straightforward. What this contains (high level): - playbooks/step-factory/README.md with conceptual overview and authoring patterns - playbooks/step-factory/templates/* (5 canonical step templates: rewrite, retrieve, rerank, synthesize, commit) - devkit/runner/index.ts: small runner that composes steps, ...

title: Add production readiness quickstart aligned with Console onboarding checklist
preview: This adds a new end-to-end “Production Readiness” quickstart intended to mirror the Console onboarding / first production launch checklist. The quickstart walks a new Hosted API user from: API key setup → first request → streaming → retries/timeouts → usage visibility → quotas/budgets guardrails → basic observabilit...
text: This adds a new end-to-end “Production Readiness” quickstart intended to mirror the Console onboarding / first production launch checklist. The quickstart walks a new Hosted API user from: API key setup → first request → streaming → retries/timeouts → usage visibility → quotas/budgets guardrails → basic observability hooks → model pinning + fallback (conceptual; uses config placeholders). Motivation: we keep seeing early prod launches fail on 401/403 setup mistakes, streaming disconnect confusion, and 429 surprises; the checklist in Console helps, but we need a canonical runnable path customers can copy/paste and deploy. Scope: adds a new quickstart directory with Python + TypeScript examples (Go is stubbed with TODO and links to the SDK guide), adds a minimal “healthcheck” endpoint pattern, structured logs, and a tiny dashboard note for usage-lag expectations. Includes guidance for w...

title: Add optional telemetry tags/headers for template adoption metrics
preview:
## Context
Product (PM-912) needs a reliable way to identify template-originated traffic for adoption KPIs (activation funnel, time-to-first-success, support deflection). Today, requests generated by the quickstarts are indistinguishable from hand-rolled integrations once users copy/paste code.

We want a convention...
text:
## Context
Product (PM-912) needs a reliable way to identify template-originated traffic for adoption KPIs (activation funnel, time-to-first-success, support deflection). Today, requests generated by the quickstarts are indistinguishable from hand-rolled integrations once users copy/paste code.

We want a convention that:
- Is **opt-in** (some users run behind strict corporate proxies / header allowlists)
- Is **privacy-safe** (no user identifiers, no workspace/customer IDs, no prompt content)
- Has **low-cardinality** values to avoid metrics blowups
- Works across **Python + TypeScript templates** and local dev/prod deployments
- Is visible in logs/traces for support debugging, but doesn’t require custom instrumentation

## What this PR does
Adds an optional telemetry tagging convention used by quickstart templates when calling the Redwood API:
- **Header:** `X-Redwood-Template` (str...

title: Pin template dependencies + add vulnerability scanning workflows
preview: Summary: This PR locks down dependency versions across the Python and TypeScript quickstart templates, adds automated vulnerability scanning in CI (PR-time + scheduled), and documents the upgrade process for template maintainers to reduce supply-chain risk. Motivation: Security review flagged unpinned transitive dep...
text: Summary: This PR locks down dependency versions across the Python and TypeScript quickstart templates, adds automated vulnerability scanning in CI (PR-time + scheduled), and documents the upgrade process for template maintainers to reduce supply-chain risk. Motivation: Security review flagged unpinned transitive dependencies and lack of automated scanning (see ENG-4190 and template security review open items). We also want template repos to adhere to the Secure SDLC addendum for templates (dependency pinning, vulnerability scanning, and a repeatable upgrade story) without introducing noisy/flake-prone CI failures. Changes included: (1) Python template: move to fully pinned requirements (direct + transitive) via pip-tools, commit compiled requirements with hashes, and add a make target to regenerate; (2) TypeScript template: ensure package-lock.json is committed and consistent, add npm...

text:
Motivation: provide a compact, low-latency RAG quickstart specifically targeted at edge/web runtime deploys using WebAssembly (WASM) and lightweight local tool adapters. Many customers building on-device or edge-hosted assistants asked for a reference that demonstrates: 1) bundling a small retriever + quantized model proxy pattern that minimizes round trips to hosted inference, 2) a WASM-based tool adapter for local deterministic utilities (e.g., date normalization, currency conversion, small knowledge transforms), and 3) a progressive retrieval strategy that returns fast first-pass answers while background-updating the context.

What this PR adds (single-string description of the changeset and rationale): Adds a new quickstart under quickstarts/wasm-edge-rag that contains an end-to-end example (README + runnable scripts) showing how to: build a tiny WASM tool adapter (Rust -> wasm32-...

text: Context: developers asked for a compact reproducible session capture and deterministic stepwise replay tool to debug RAG agents at the edge where reproducing live incidents is costly. Motivation: provide a lightweight CLI quickstart that captures tool calls, prompt inputs/outputs, and intermediate KV cache snapshots in a compressed, schema-vetted fixture format, plus a playback runner that can step through a session with assertions and telemetry hooks. This PR adds an end-to-end example, docs, tests, CI, and minimal telemetry schema. title: Add RAG local policy enforcer and contract-linting quickstart
preview: Motivation: Teams building retrieval-augmented agents often struggle to enforce tool-contract compatibility and structured-output schemas during local development. This quickstart adds a small runnable sandbox and a contract-lint that validates tool calls, JSON schemas, and policy rules before deployment. It targets...
text:
Motivation: Teams building retrieval-augmented agents often struggle to enforce tool-contract compatibility and structured-output schemas during local development. This quickstart adds a small runnable sandbox and a contract-lint that validates tool calls, JSON schemas, and policy rules before deployment. It targets developer productivity and pre-production safety checks to reduce integration bugs when moving from local authoring to Redwood-hosted or dedicated deployments.

What this PR contains: Adds a new quickstart under quickstarts/rag-policy-enforcer that includes: a) a lightweight node+python devserver that runs a stubbed RAG agent and tool adapters, b) a policy-enforcer binary (Go) that loads envelope rules (allow/deny/transform) and reports violations, c) a contract-lint CLI that checks structured-output contracts against JSON Schema and example traces, d) telemetry probe pres...
